mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with t-stat > 2.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment growth is driven by labor force participation changes rather than job losses.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates demand destruction driven by price shocks, which reduces corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually while RSAFS remains flat, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the strategy is negative over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive equity returns during supply-constrained periods where input bottlenecks offset output gains, or rolling Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth alongside stable federal funds rates indicates robust economic expansion without immediate tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surpassing 1.4 million units signals strong residential investment demand and construction sector activity, which spills over into broader economic growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts remain above threshold while S&P 500 delivers negative 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal reverses during periods of restrictive mortgage rates.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding employment and rising household income, which sustains consumer demand and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive equity returns when driven solely by government sector hiring, or the signal information coefficient remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth concurrent with elevated consumer price inflation creates a stagflationary environment that compresses corporate earnings and discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns when inflation is driven by transitory supply shocks rather than demand pressures, or strategy drawdown exceeds 15% during triggered periods.
